title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
pattern: Linked List â€” Two Pointer Gap Technique
coreIdea: Remove the nth node from the end in a single pass by first advancing one pointer n steps ahead, then moving both pointers together until the lead pointer reaches the end, leaving the trailing pointer just before the node to remove.
recognitionCues: asks to remove a node counted from the END of the list
single-pass O(n) solution expected instead of two passes (one to find length, one to remove)
approach: Use a dummy node before head to simplify edge cases. Advance a fast pointer n+1 steps from dummy. Then move both slow (from dummy) and fast pointers together until fast reaches null. Slow now points to the node just before the target; unlink the target by setting slow.next = slow.next.next.
